name: Lime Marmalade
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose fruit that has not been waxed. Place the fruit in a large kettle and add enough water to cover.
2. Bring the mixture to a boil and simmer until the fruit can be pierced with a fork.
3. Remove the fruit, reserving the water it was boiled in. Cool the fruit, cut lengthwise into quarters, remove seeds, and slice as thinly as possible.
4. Return the fruit, along with any accumulated juices, to the reserved water. Measure the total liquid.
5. For every one cup of liquid, add three-quarters of a cup of sugar. Bring the mixture to a boil and continue boiling until it reaches the jelly stage (220 degrees F on a candy thermometer).
6. Pour into sterilized half-pint or pint jars, seal, and process according to the jar manufacturer's instructions.
